title: Season rhythm and stop points phase
summary: Sixtieth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-1 wave tightens stop-point rhythm and return-session confidence through the current season shell instead of adding a new recap or planner surface.

execution_order.why_now: Stop-point confidence is the next high-leverage gain once the season shell settles.
The current shell can communicate better pause and resume beats without growing.
The season should feel easier to carry without becoming more managerial.
A small follow-on can make returning after a break even easier.
A better resume seam is valuable for longer real play sessions.
The result should stay light and game-first.

guardrails: Do not add a separate recap screen.
Do not add another planner or task log.
Reuse existing strip, board, wrap, expedition, journal, and map seams.
Let the top strip carry stop permission while the atlas keeps the direct next-action line.
For the route-resume follow-on, reuse the existing field-request notice strip on title resume instead of adding another reminder surface.
acceptance_focus: The season is easier to stop and resume.
The shell remains compact and calm.
Lane 1 improves rhythm without system growth.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-1.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-season-rhythm-and-stop-points-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 1's next long-push follow-on after the board-calm wave: tighten stop-point rhythm and return-session confidence through the current season shell without adding a separate recap or planner surface.
